headline: European Payments Initiative CEO says Trump fears are boosting its appeal
summary: Fears that President Donald Trump's administration could limit Europe's access to U.S. payments infrastructure are boosting regional adoption of a competing ‌European payment platform, the CEO of the European Payments Initiative (EPI) told Reuters.  The Brussels-based company has developed Wero ‌to serve as an alternative to Mastercard, Visa and Apple Pay, which now dominate in-store payments in Europe.  Founded in 2020 by a group ​of 16 major European banks, including BNP Paribas and Deutsche Bank, and payments providers, it has since grown to 45 members, with fintechs Mollie, Worldpay, and N26 among the companies to have joined in recent months.

raw.headline: European Payments Initiative CEO says Trump fears are boosting its appeal
raw.summary: Fears that President Donald Trump's administration could limit Europe's access to U.S. payments infrastructure are boosting regional adoption of a competing ‌European payment platform, the CEO of the European Payments Initiative (EPI) told Reuters.  The Brussels-based company has developed Wero ‌to serve as an alternative to Mastercard, Visa and Apple Pay, which now dominate in-store payments in Europe.  Founded in 2020 by a group ​of 16 major European banks, including BNP Paribas and Deutsche Bank, and payments providers, it has since grown to 45 members, with fintechs Mollie, Worldpay, and N26 among the companies to have joined in recent months.
